rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
The aim is that students develop general communicative competence and learn to apply this productively in authentic spoken interactions. Eikawa
I is a practical course in which students practice skills to express themselves in a foreign language and negotiate meaning in communicative
conversational settings.
goals: To develop English communication ability, enphasizing speaking and listening skills;
To build awareness of the socially appropriate use of language according to context;
To develop cultural understanding as it relates to English language use.

plan.text:
CONTENT
Course introduction (syllabus, course requirements, evaluation);
Topic: introducing and getting to know each other;
Language function: introducing oneself and inquiring about others; review of
basic question forms.
EVALUATION:
Introduction and explanation of the KU rubric for assessing small‑group
interaction.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Revision of dialog strategies introduced in the first session.

CONTENT:
Topic: About me / About you (talking about oneself in terms of personality,
hobbies, origins, likes and dislikes, strengths and weaknesses; inquiring about
others);
Listening activity and conversation‑based activities; socially appropriate
strategies (reactions and responses).
EVALUATION:
Continued evaluation of in‑class activities.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Revision of vocabulary, expressions and dialog patterns.

CONTENT:
Conversation topic: Health (talking about one's health condition and lifestyle)
Listening activity;
Practical conversation activities;
Applied grammar review from the 4th and 5th classes: count and Noncount
Nouns / Quantifiers.
EVALUATION:
Continued evaluation of in‑class activities.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Revision of vocabulary, expressions and dialogs.

CONTENT:
Conversation topic: Dreams;
Language functions covered: discussing realistic plans as well as unrealistic
ones;
Applied grammar points covered: first and second conditionals.
EVALUATION:
Continued evaluation of in‑class activities.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Revision of vocabulary, expressions and dialogs.

CONTENT:
Conversation topic: Opinions (miscellaneous approaches to how to express
one’s opinion appropriately in an English‑speaking context);
Specific language functions covered: main and relative clauses (“I can’t believe
how ∼”, “I think that ∼”, “I think it is interesting that ∼, [etc.]);
EVALUATION:
Continued evaluation of in‑class activities.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Revision of vocabulary, expressions and dialogs.

Class 11: December 18th, 2025
CONTENT:
Conversation topic: Where I am from / Where I live (talking about the social and
economical aspects / assets & drawbacks of one's living environment;
comparing cities and places).
EVALUATION:
Continued evaluation of in‑class activities.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Revision of vocabulary, expressions and dialogs.

CONTENT:
Conversation topic: The Global Crisis (talking about the various challenges
taking place in the four corners of the world and possible solutions to them);
Applied grammar points: modals + passive form (“more should be done to ∼”
[etc.]; modals.
EVALUATION:
Continued evaluation of in‑class activities.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Worksheet‑based revision of specific topic‑related vocabulary.

CONTENT:
Conversation topic: Work (talking about one's country's professional
environment, miscellaneous countries' work cultures as well as new corporate
cultures, and envisioning one's future professional life);
Language function covered: stressing contrasts / comparing;
Applied grammar points covered: might and may.
EVALUATION:
Continued evaluation of in‑class activities.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Revision of vocabulary, expressions and dialogs.

CONTENT:
Overall review of the topics and speaking strategies covered throughout the
semester;
Explanation provided regarding the upcoming Final Speaking Test;
Group Practice in preparation for the Final Exam.
EVALUATION:
Continued evaluation of in‑class activities.
OUTSIDE WORK:
Speaking Test Preparation – preferably in groups of 2 or 3 students.

CONTENT:
Final Evaluation: Group A – Group Speaking Test

CONTENT:
Final Evaluation: Group B – Group Speaking Test

textbooks:
All course materials will be provided by the teacher. For students' reference, most materials will be based on WORD LINK,
Developing English Fluency Level 1 Third Edition (National Geographic Learning). Follow‑up materials from alternate sources will
also be used and provided.
prereq: This class is available to all first‑year students and is a requirement for graduation.
prep:
Students will be required to review conversations along with the applied language techniques, functions and vocabulary studied
in class in order to reinforce their competence, prepare for future lessons and in‑class assessment.
教科
teachers: ◎ ジョナサン ヘルムリンガー

extra.label: 区分1
extra.text: 平成20年度以降入学生／令和６年度以降入学生

extra.label: 区分2
extra.text: 共通教育）初年次科目／国際コミュニケーション科目基軸英語

extra.label: 履修における注意点
extra.text: １年次に必修。１外国語（英語）履修上限年間10単位。卒業にはⅠとⅡ両方が必要。

extra.label: 資格等
extra.text: 教免－省令で定める科目：外国語コミュニケーション

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
人類の文化・社会・自然に関する知識
論理的思考力
語学に関するリテラシー
表現力
コミュニケーション力
協働実践力

extra.label: 教員の実務経験の有無
